name: Physique
rule: Represents your character's raw physical strength, muscle mass, and biological durability. It is the base die used when rolling for any physical feats, and acts as your static "Wall" defense against heavy attacks or crushing force.
guide: Look, the galaxy is mostly made of cold vacuums, heavy rocks, and people who want to hit you with heavy rocks. Physique is your meat. If you don't have enough meat, you are going to get squished. It's that simple.

name: Athletics
rule: Used for lifting heavy objects, climbing sheer surfaces, swimming, sprinting across hazardous terrain, or physically forcing a jammed blast door open.
guide: The art of moving your meat from Point A to Point B before Point A explodes. A lot of rookies think they just need a big gun to survive, right up until the bridge collapses and they realize they can't do a pull-up. Don't skip leg day in zero-G.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You complete the physical task with visible effort — a clumsy vault, a strained climb, a sprint that leaves you winded. It works, barely.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Clean execution. The climb is smooth, the leap clears the gap, the sprint covers ground without wasted motion. Competent and efficient.
narrativeTiers.legendary: The kind of physical performance that makes people stop and watch. You clear obstacles that should require equipment, cover ground that should take twice as long, or hold a position that should be impossible.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your body moves like a machine built for exactly this task. Witnesses question whether you are enhanced. Walls become ladders. Gaps become nothing.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Physics bends around your effort. You outrun vehicles on foot, climb surfaces with no handholds, or swim against currents that would drown a boat. People do not believe the reports.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Mythic physicality. You move through the environment as if gravity, friction, and material strength are suggestions. Legends are built around what you just did.

name: Brawl
rule: Used for unarmed combat, grappling, shoving, or using improvised weapons (like a chair or a glass). When you attack with Brawl, it natively targets the enemy's Physique (Defense).
guide: So, you forgot to charge your blaster, you dropped your vibro-knife, and now there is a very angry Trandoshan in your personal space. Brawl is the science of putting your fist through their teeth before they bite your nose off. It's not pretty, it's not elegant, but neither is dying in an alleyway.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You land a glancing blow or establish a shaky grip. The opponent feels it, but is not meaningfully impaired. You break down a flimsy barrier with effort.
narrativeTiers.masterful: A solid strike lands where intended, a grapple locks in clean, or a barrier gives way with authority. The outcome is decisive and professional.
narrativeTiers.legendary: The hit restructures the fight. Bones shift, opponents rethink their life choices, reinforced doors buckle. The raw physical violence is precise and devastating.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You hit with force that should not come from an organic body. Durasteel dents. Opponents are lifted off their feet. Witnesses flinch.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Your strikes carry the weight of industrial machinery. Structures crack. Armored opponents feel every joule through their plating. The ground shakes.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You punch through things that should not be punchable. Blast doors. Vehicle hulls. The line between unarmed combat and demolition has ceased to exist.

name: Endure
rule: This is your primary Reaction against physical trauma. When targeted by an attack that targets your Physique (like a Heavy Weapon, an explosion, or a crushing hazard), roll Physique + Endure to meet or beat the enemy's Static Power to soak the damage.
guide: Sometimes, despite your best efforts, you are going to get shot, blown up, or thrown out of a moving speeder. Endure is the discipline of looking at a broken rib, spitting out a little blood, and deciding that you simply don't have the time to die today.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You absorb the impact but feel every bit of it. You stay standing, but just barely. The pain is real and present.
narrativeTiers.masterful: You take the hit and keep moving. The damage is real but contained — your body absorbs what it needs to and routes around the rest.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You shrug off punishment that should have dropped you. Witnesses wonder if you felt it at all. Your body processes trauma with mechanical efficiency.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You walk through damage that would kill most people. Burns close. Impacts bounce. Your body seems to reject the concept of being harmed.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You stand in conditions that destroy equipment. Explosions wash over you. Toxic atmospheres fail to register. Your survival defies medical explanation.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You endure the unsurvivable. Vacuum. Direct energy weapon fire. Structural collapse. Your body simply refuses to stop functioning. Medics will argue about how for years.

name: Melee
rule: Used for wielding dedicated close-quarters weapons like Vibro-Axes, stun batons, or swords. Always uses Physique as the base Attribute unless a weapon trait or character ability explicitly states otherwise. Natively targets the enemy's Physique (Defense).
guide: Blaster gas runs out. Power packs fry. But a heavy piece of sharpened metal? That is eternal. Melee is the art of swinging something heavy and sharp at the other guy without accidentally amputating your own leg in the process.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The blade connects but without authority — a shallow cut, a deflected thrust. The weapon work is functional but unrefined.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Clean, purposeful strikes that land where intended. The weapon becomes an extension of will — parries flow into counters, footwork creates openings.
narrativeTiers.legendary: The weapon moves faster than the eye can track. Every strike is surgical. Opponents with superior numbers find themselves outmatched by technique alone.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your bladework transcends training. You cut through defenses that should hold, strike angles that should not exist, and move with a fluidity that borders on choreography.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: The weapon seems alive. You engage multiple opponents simultaneously with the casual precision of someone solving a simple puzzle. Armor is an inconvenience.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Mastery beyond mastery. You cut through materials that should resist your weapon. Every movement is perfect. This is the swordwork that creates legends and ends dynasties.

name: Heavy Weapons
rule: Used for firing massive, squad-support weapons (like rotary cannons or the DLT-19) or launching heavy ordnance. Because of the sheer recoil and weight, these weapons use Physique for their Control die instead of Reflex.
guide: If you are holding a gun that is larger than you are, it wants to fly backward out of your hands the second you pull the trigger. This discipline is purely about having the sheer muscle mass required to hold a rotary cannon steady while making the entire zip-code in front of you disappear.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The weapon fires but the recoil fights you. Rounds land in the general area. Suppression is achieved through volume, not precision.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Controlled bursts hit where intended. The weapon is tamed — recoil managed, traverse smooth, impact concentrated on the target area.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You wield the heavy weapon like it weighs nothing. Sustained fire walks across targets with surgical precision. Structures buckle under concentrated impact.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You operate the weapon at a level its designers did not anticipate. Rate of fire, accuracy, and sustained output exceed the platform rating. The weapon becomes an extension of pure destructive will.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You extract performance from the weapon that should cause mechanical failure. Every round finds its mark. The destruction is systematic and absolute.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You redefine what the weapon is capable of. Single-handedly suppressing battalion-level threats, threading rounds through gaps in heavy armor, or sustaining fire that reshapes the terrain. Ordnance officers will study what you did.

name: Grit
rule: Represents your character's mental fortitude, pain tolerance, sheer stubbornness, and ability to keep a cool head when everything goes wrong. It is the base die used for surviving harsh environments, overpowering beasts, and acting as your "Core" defense against psychological, social, or Force-based pressure.
guide: Some people survive the Rim because they are fast. Some survive because they are covered in thick armor. But the ones who live the longest? They survive because they are simply too stubborn to die. Grit is the fine art of looking at a completely hopeless situation and refusing to blink.

name: Beast Handling
rule: The practice of taming, riding, or intimidating non-sentient creatures (blurrgs, dewbacks, rontos, or worse).
guide: Fauna in this galaxy generally falls into two categories: things that want to eat you, and things you can ride to get away from the things that want to eat you. This discipline is about convincing a three-ton, carnivorous lizard that you are the boss, usually by staring it right in the eye and absolutely refusing to show fear.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The creature obeys reluctantly — it follows the command but with visible hesitation. The bond is thin and the creature is one scare away from bolting.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The creature responds with purpose and trust. Commands are followed cleanly. The animal works with you, not despite you.
narrativeTiers.legendary: Perfect communion. The creature anticipates your intent before you signal. You ride, direct, or calm animals that others cannot approach.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: The creature treats you as pack alpha without question. Wild animals that should flee or attack instead defer. Your presence commands the animal kingdom.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You communicate with creatures on a level that defies zoological understanding. Predators stand down. Herds part. Beasts perform tasks they were never trained for.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You command the loyalty of creatures that should be uncontrollable — apex predators, swarm organisms, beasts of legend. They follow you not out of fear, but recognition.

name: Control
rule: (Requires Force Sensitivity.) The mastery of channeling the Force inward to regulate the body and spirit. In Narrative Scenes, this grants the permission to attempt superhuman feats of bio-feedback—such as surviving in a hard vacuum for a few minutes, entering a deep healing trance to knit broken bones, or purging lethal toxins from the blood. In Combat Scenes, this deep focus is fragmented by the chaos of battle, requiring the user to channel it through specific Gambits or Maneuvers to achieve localized effects like absorbing blaster fire with a bare hand or pushing the body to supernatural speeds. Non-sensitives cannot invest in this discipline.
guide: I've seen a lot of tough guys in the Rim. Scars, cybernetics, pain suppressors. But I once saw a guy in robes take a point-blank blaster bolt to the palm, close his eyes, and just... breathe it in. No tech. No armor. Just some spooky inner calm. If you aren't born with that weird monk magic, don't even try it. Just buy a thicker vest.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: A moment of inner calm. You steady your breathing, push back a wave of pain, or clear a single distracting thought. The Force responds, but barely.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Deep focus takes hold. You regulate your heartbeat, suppress a toxin, or enter a shallow meditative state while the world rages around you. The Force sustains you.
narrativeTiers.legendary: Your body obeys your will completely. You suppress injuries that should be debilitating, purge poisons that should be lethal, or enter a healing trance that accelerates recovery far beyond natural limits.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You exert control over biological processes that are normally involuntary. Wounds close visibly. Broken bones set themselves. Your body becomes a vessel for the Force, rewriting damage in real time.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You survive conditions that should be instantly fatal through pure Force-mediated bio-control. Hard vacuum, lethal radiation, systemic organ failure — your will overrides your biology.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Transcendent self-mastery. You enter states that blur the line between life and death — suspended animation lasting decades, surviving dismemberment through Force-sustained biology, or channeling the Force through your body with such intensity that you become a living conduit. This is Darth Sion refusing to die.

name: Intimidate
rule: The application of coercion, fear, or physical menace to bully a target into compliance. When used in social combat, it natively targets the enemy's Resolve.
guide: You don't always need to shoot someone to win a fight. Sometimes, you just need to look at them and calmly explain that shooting you is the last mistake they will ever make. It's about projecting pure, unadulterated consequence. Pro-tip: a quiet, dead-eyed growl usually works way better than screaming.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The target hesitates. They are not convinced, but they are no longer comfortable. A seed of doubt has been planted.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The target takes you seriously. Their body language shifts — they reassess the situation, recalculate the odds, and visibly reconsider their position.
narrativeTiers.legendary: The target is shaken to their core. Hardened criminals reconsider their career choices. Trained soldiers question their orders. Your presence fills the room like a physical force.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your mere attention breaks people. Targets comply not because they calculated the odds, but because something primal in their brain screamed at them to submit. Crowds part.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Your reputation arrives before you do. Targets who have only heard stories about you feel the weight of your displeasure. Entire rooms fall silent when you enter.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You project an aura of absolute consequence. Warlords kneel. Armies hesitate. Your will imposes itself on the environment like gravity — not through words, but through sheer existential pressure. This is Vader walking onto a rebel ship.

name: Resolve
rule: Your primary Reaction against mental, social, or Force-based attacks. Used to maintain your composure and firewall your mind against an intimidation attempt, a Jedi mind trick, or overwhelming terror. (Note: For Droids, this represents firewall integrity and resistance to ion/slicing attacks).
guide: The galaxy is absolutely full of smooth-talking grifters, terrifying warlords, and weird wizards who wave their hands and tell you what to think. Resolve is your mental blast door. It's the little voice in your head that says, "No, I don't think I will give you my ship, and no, I am not actually scared of your glowing sword."

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You hold your ground, but it costs you. The fear, the pressure, the manipulation — you feel all of it. You just choose not to break.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The attempt to break you finds no purchase. Your mind is a closed door. You acknowledge the pressure and dismiss it with quiet certainty.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You are unmovable. Interrogation techniques fail. Force probes slide off your consciousness. Your willpower is a physical barrier that others cannot breach.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your mental fortitude radiates outward. Allies near you feel steadier. Enemies who attempt to break you find their own confidence eroding instead.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You resist forms of mental assault that should be irresistible. Deep Force mind-probes, chemical interrogation, prolonged psychological torture — your will outlasts them all.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Your resolve defines reality. You reject outcomes that should be inevitable — refusing to die when the body has failed, maintaining identity when the mind should be shattered, holding your ground against forces that reshape the wills of nations. This is the mental fortress that even Sith Lords cannot crack.

name: Survival
rule: The knowledge required for tracking targets through the wilderness, foraging for food and water, navigating planetary hazards, and building temporary shelters.
guide: If you crash land on Tatooine or Hoth, the impact probably won't kill you. The twin suns, the freezing nights, and the sheer lack of water will. Survival is the unglamorous, miserable work of knowing which desert grub won't poison you and how to sleep in the dirt without freezing to death.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You find basic shelter, identify an edible plant, or follow an obvious trail. The wilderness does not kill you today, but it is not impressed.
narrativeTiers.masterful: You read the environment fluently. You track targets across difficult terrain, find water in a desert, navigate by stars, or build a shelter that actually works.
narrativeTiers.legendary: The wilderness becomes your domain. You track targets across surfaces that hold no prints, predict weather shifts by smell, or survive in environments that kill equipped expeditions.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You exist in hostile environments as if you were native fauna. You track across running water, predict geological events, and extract resources from terrain that appears barren.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You survive in environments that should be categorically lethal without specialized equipment. Your understanding of natural systems borders on prescience.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You thrive where survival is theoretically impossible. Trackless wastes, toxic hellscapes, the deep void between stars — you find a way. Expeditions are planned around your capabilities. Your survival instinct has transcended human limits.

name: Reflex
rule: Represents your character's agility, reaction time, fine motor skills, and hand-eye coordination. It is the base die used when rolling for acrobatic feats, ranged accuracy, and acts as your active "Ghost" defense when trying to dodge incoming attacks or area-of-effect hazards.
guide: Speed kills, but more importantly, speed keeps you from being killed. Heavy armor is great if you enjoy walking like a pregnant bantha, but the absolute best way to survive a firefight is to simply not be standing where the blaster bolt lands.

name: Evasion
rule: This is your primary Reaction against ranged attacks and hazards. When targeted by a blaster, a swinging lightsaber, or a thermal detonator, roll Reflex + Evasion to meet or beat the enemy's Static Power.
guide: The most valuable skill in the Outer Rim is the ability to abruptly be somewhere else. Ducking, diving, rolling into a pile of garbage — whatever it takes. It lacks dignity, sure, but dignity is what they write on the tombstones of slow people.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You dodge, but just barely. The blast singes your coat, the debris clips your shoulder. You are alive because you moved, not because you moved well.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Clean evasion. You are not where the danger lands. Your movement is economical — no wasted motion, no dramatic dive. You simply are not there.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You move through a field of hazards untouched. Blaster fire, falling debris, explosions — you weave through it all with an almost insulting casualness.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your evasion defies reaction time. You dodge things you should not be able to see coming. Witnesses swear you moved before the trigger was pulled.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You exist in the gaps between danger. Automated targeting systems cannot track you. Area-effect weapons find you in the one safe pocket. Your movement is precognitive.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You are untouchable. Concentrated fire from multiple sources finds empty air. Explosions detonate around a you-shaped void. The universe itself seems to route danger around your position.

name: Piloting
rule: Used for operating vehicles of all sizes, from repulsorlift speeders to light freighters and starfighters. Required for pulling evasive maneuvers, navigating treacherous asteroid fields, or punching the hyperdrive while under heavy fire.
guide: Anyone can push a throttle forward. Piloting is the art of pushing the throttle forward while four Imperial TIEs are trying to turn your thrusters into molten slag, your astromech is screaming, and you're casually threading the needle through a collapsing mine shaft.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You keep the vehicle moving in roughly the right direction. The ride is rough, the turns are wide, but you do not crash.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Confident, competent piloting. You thread gaps, execute evasive maneuvers, and handle the vehicle as if it were part of your body.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You fly the vehicle beyond its rated performance envelope. Maneuvers that should cause structural failure execute cleanly. Pursuit vehicles cannot keep up.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You extract performance from the vehicle that the manufacturer did not know was possible. Mechanics will study your flight recorder data and argue about the physics.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You pilot as if the vehicle has no limitations. Atmospheric re-entry in a shuttle rated for orbital only. Threading an asteroid field at full throttle. The vehicle survives because you willed it.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Transcendent piloting. You fly through conditions that should destroy the vehicle and the pilot. The Kessel Run. The Death Star trench. Atmospheric entry without shields. Your name becomes synonymous with the impossible.

name: Ranged
rule: Used for firing pistols, carbines, standard rifles, and thrown weapons. Relies on hand-eye coordination rather than brute strength. Natively targets the enemy's Defense Resistance.
guide: Hokey religions and ancient melee weapons are great for dramatic tension, but there is a profound, unmatched joy in putting a searing hole through a bounty hunter from thirty paces before he even unclips his holster. Keep your piece clean and your eyes open.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The shot lands, but not where you wanted it. A grazing hit, a near miss that still achieves the goal. Functional, not elegant.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The shot goes exactly where you aimed. Center mass, weak point, or called target — the bolt finds its mark with professional precision.
narrativeTiers.legendary: Shooting that makes other marksmen stop and stare. Impossible angles, moving targets at extreme range, or multiple targets in rapid succession. Every shot is a statement.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You shoot with a precision that should require targeting computers. Moving targets at absurd range, ricochet shots off reflective surfaces, or disabling shots on targets you cannot directly see.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Your accuracy transcends the weapon platform. You hit targets the weapon should not be able to reach. You shoot around corners through instinct. Targeting droids are jealous.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Every shot is perfect. You hit what you intend to hit, when you intend to hit it, from any position, at any range. Your marksmanship becomes the standard against which all others are measured. This is the shot heard across the galaxy.

name: Skulduggery
rule: Used for sleight of hand, picking physical locks, planting tracking devices, or concealing small hold-out weapons (like a Q2) from pat-downs and security scanners.
guide: The galaxy is completely full of locked boxes and people who violently disagree with your right to have what's inside them. Skulduggery is the noble, time-honored tradition of aggressive, undocumented borrowing.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The lock opens, but it took too long. The planted device is in place, but not perfectly concealed. The sleight of hand works, but a sharp eye might have caught it.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Clean work. The lock yields silently, the device is planted without a trace, the palmed object disappears completely. Professional-grade execution.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You defeat security systems that were designed to be undefeatable. You plant objects on targets who are actively watching for it. Your hands move faster than eyes can follow.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your manual dexterity operates at a level that defies human physiology. You pick locks by touch alone in total darkness. You plant tracking devices during a handshake.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Security systems designed by the best minds in the galaxy fall to your touch like paper locks. You steal objects from secured display cases while guards are watching the case.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You operate at the intersection of art and crime. You steal things that should be unstealable, bypass systems that should be unbypassable, and leave no trace that you were ever there. Your work is studied in security academies as cautionary tales.

name: Stealth
rule: Used for moving silently, blending into crowds, sticking to the shadows, and ambushing targets before the Initiative is rolled. Opposed by the enemy's Sense or Investigation disciplines.
guide: Here is a simple mathematical equation for you: If they don't know you are in the room, they cannot shoot you. Stick to the vents, walk on the balls of your feet, and remember that breathing loudly is a luxury you currently cannot afford.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You avoid notice, but not by much. A shadow that almost blended, a footstep that almost did not register. One more second of scrutiny and you would have been caught.
narrativeTiers.masterful: You are invisible to casual observation. You move through guarded spaces without triggering alerts, blend into crowds seamlessly, and leave rooms without anyone noticing you were there.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You disappear in plain sight. Active search teams walk past your position. Security systems fail to register your presence. You are a ghost in the machine.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You move through environments that should make stealth impossible — open terrain, brightly lit corridors, rooms full of alert observers — and remain unseen.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Dedicated sensor arrays, trained hunter-killer teams, and Force-sensitive searchers cannot locate you. You exist outside the perceptual framework of everyone around you.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You are functionally invisible. You move through the galaxy leaving no footprint, no record, no memory. People who looked directly at you cannot recall your face. You are the absence of evidence.

name: Wits
rule: Represents your character's intellect, memory, technical aptitude, and analytical speed. It is the base die used for slicing, repairing gear, treating wounds, and reading the tactical layout of a battlefield.
guide: You can be as strong as a Wookiee and as fast as a swoop bike, but if you don't know which wire stops the thermal detonator from turning you into a fine red mist, you're going to have a very short career. Wits is what separates the professionals from the casualties.

name: Investigation
rule: The practice of searching a room, applying deductive reasoning, gathering local rumors, or finding the hidden compartment in a smuggler's hold.
guide: The Empire loves hiding things in plain sight, and criminals love hiding things under the floorboards. Investigation is the art of tearing a room apart with your eyes before you tear it apart with your hands.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You notice something out of place — a scratched lock, an odd stain, a nervous glance. It is a thread, but you have not pulled it yet.
narrativeTiers.masterful: You reconstruct events from evidence. The crime scene tells you a story. The discrepancies in the testimony become obvious. You know what happened and roughly when.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You see what no one else sees. Hidden compartments, micro-expressions, patterns in seemingly random data — your analysis reveals truths that were meant to stay buried.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You reconstruct events from evidence so fragmentary that others would not recognize it as evidence at all. A scuff mark tells you the weight, gait, and species of the person who made it.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Your investigative intuition borders on prescience. You solve cases from evidence that does not appear to exist. You predict the next move of targets you have never met.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You perceive the truth of any situation with absolute clarity. No deception survives your scrutiny. No hidden motive remains hidden. You see through lies, facades, and conspiracies the way others see through glass.

name: Medicine
rule: The knowledge required for treating severe wounds, diagnosing exotic poisons, performing field surgery, or properly applying a stim-pack to clear a Trauma Box.
guide: So your buddy just took a fist-sized chunk of shrapnel to the thigh. You have three minutes, a roll of mesh-tape, and a half-empty bacta tube. Medicine is the discipline of making sure your friend keeps their leg instead of bleeding out on the hangar floor.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You stop the bleeding, stabilize the patient, or identify the poison. The treatment is rough, but the patient is no longer getting worse.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Professional-grade field medicine. You set bones correctly, close wounds cleanly, administer the right dosage, and diagnose conditions accurately under pressure.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You perform medical feats that would impress a fully equipped surgical team, using field supplies and improvised tools. Patients who should be dead are stabilized and recovering.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You treat injuries that should require a bacta tank with field supplies. Your understanding of physiology borders on prescient — you anticipate complications before they manifest.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You restore function to bodies that have suffered catastrophic damage. Limbs that should be amputated are saved. Toxins that should be lethal are neutralized through improvised chemistry.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Miracle worker. You bring patients back from conditions that medical science considers irreversible. Your treatments redefine what field medicine can accomplish. Surgical droids request your consultation.

name: Sense
rule: (Requires Force Sensitivity.) The mastery of expanding one's awareness beyond the physical senses, feeling the connective tissue of the living Force. In Narrative Scenes, this grants the permission to experience profound visions, read the lingering emotional echoes in a room, or sense the life signs of creatures miles away. In Combat Scenes, the noise of violence narrows this connection, requiring specific Gambits or Maneuvers to anticipate an enemy's strike, deflect incoming fire blindfolded, or perceive the immediate tactical intentions of an opponent. Non-sensitives cannot invest in this discipline.
guide: A good smuggler relies on their gut. A Jedi relies on the fundamental connective tissue of the universe whispering the future into their ear. It's wildly unfair. You and I have to check our corners and watch our six. These wizards just close their eyes and know exactly how many bounty hunters are waiting behind the blast door.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: A whisper from the Force — a vague sense of danger, a feeling that something living is nearby, a half-formed impression of emotional residue in a room.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The Force opens a clear channel. You sense the number and disposition of life forms nearby, read the emotional state of a target, or feel the echo of recent violence in a location.
narrativeTiers.legendary: Your awareness extends far beyond your physical senses. You perceive hidden threats, read surface thoughts of willing or unguarded minds, and sense disturbances in the Force across planetary distances.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You perceive the Force as a living web connecting all things. You track individuals across cities through their Force signature, read the history of objects through psychometric impression, or sense intentions before they form.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Your perception transcends physical space. You sense events in other star systems, read the deep motivations buried beneath layers of mental shielding, or perceive timelines of possible futures with startling clarity.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Omniscient awareness. You perceive the galaxy as a single interconnected organism. You see across space and time — past echoes, present realities, and branching futures. This is Yoda sensing Order 66 across the galaxy. The Force hides nothing from you.

name: Tactics
rule: The application of battlefield analysis. Used for rolling Initiative at the start of combat, deducing enemy weaknesses, or identifying the best position to lay an ambush.
guide: Shooting first is great, but shooting from the high ground behind a durasteel barricade is better. Tactics is the ability to look at a room full of heavily armed hostiles and instantly figure out which explosive barrel to shoot to make the problem go away.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You identify the obvious tactical elements — cover positions, firing lanes, the numerical balance. Basic competence that keeps you from walking into an ambush.
narrativeTiers.masterful: You read the battlefield fluently. You identify the optimal engagement plan, predict enemy movements, and position your team for maximum advantage.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You see the battle three moves ahead. Your tactical analysis accounts for variables others do not even notice. Enemies find themselves outmaneuvered before they realize the fight has started.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your tactical mind operates at a speed and depth that borders on computational. You process battlefield variables in real time, adjusting plans faster than the enemy can react.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You devise strategies that account for every contingency. Enemies with superior numbers, equipment, and position find themselves defeated by plans they cannot comprehend until it is too late.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Strategic genius that reshapes conflicts. You see the entire theater of war as a single system and manipulate it with surgical precision. Battles are won before they begin. This is Thrawn studying the art on the wall.

name: Tech
rule: The expertise required for slicing (hacking) secure terminals, bypassing electronic locks, repairing starships, and calculating hyperspace jumps.
guide: Everything in this galaxy runs on dying, hundred-year-old computer code and sparking wires. If you can speak to the machines, you own the galaxy. Just remember: slicing a bank account makes you rich; slicing an Imperial security door keeps you alive.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You get the system to respond, but it is not pretty. The splice is noisy, the repair holds but wobbles, the bypass trips a minor alarm. Functional, not elegant.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Clean technical work. The terminal opens, the hyperdrive hums back to life, the door unseals without a trace. Your code is tight and your solder joints are clean.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You make technology do things it was not designed to do. You slice military-grade encryption, jury-rig a hyperdrive from scrap parts, or reprogram a hostile droid mid-combat.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You interface with technology at a level that borders on intuition. Systems respond to your touch as if they want to be fixed. Code writes itself under your fingers. Hardware bends to your will.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You rewrite the rules of what technology can do. You splice systems that were designed to be un-spliceable, repair damage that should require a full factory rebuild, or extract data from hardware that has been physically destroyed.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You transcend the limitations of existing technology. You build things that should not work, hack systems that should not exist, and repair the irreparable. Your technical genius is studied as theoretical breakthrough. This is the engineer who builds the impossible.

name: Presence
rule: Represents your character's sheer force of personality, social gravity, and command over a room. It is the base die used for lying, negotiating, leading troops, and navigating the treacherous social waters of the Outer Rim.
guide: A blaster can get you out of a room, but a good smile and a confident lie can get you the keys to the ship, the codes to the vault, and an escort to the landing pad. In this galaxy, if you aren't the one talking, you're the one being talked about—usually right before the airlock opens.

name: Alter
rule: (Requires Force Sensitivity.) The mastery of projecting the Force outward to manipulate the physical world and the minds of others. In Narrative Scenes, this grants the permission to perform massive feats of telekinesis like lifting a sunken starfighter from a swamp, parting a sea of flames, or fundamentally rewriting a weak-willed guard's memories. In Combat Scenes, the immense concentration required is constantly broken by the chaos of battle, restricting the user to specific Gambits or Maneuvers to hurl heavy debris, shove enemies with kinetic force, or subtly trick an opponent's mind in the heat of the moment. Non-sensitives cannot invest in this discipline.
guide: Guns jam. Power packs run dry. But the guy who can choke you from across the room without lifting a single finger? He never has to reload. It's the ultimate trump card. If you see someone wearing bathrobes wave their hand at you, don't ask questions. Just start shooting. Or better yet, run.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: A nudge through the Force — a small object slides across a table, a weak-willed mind accepts a simple suggestion, a gust of Force-driven wind stirs dust.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The Force answers with authority. You move objects too heavy to lift, plant compelling suggestions in resistant minds, or manipulate your environment with precision and purpose.
narrativeTiers.legendary: Your will reshapes the physical world. You hurl heavy objects with devastating force, rewrite the short-term memories of strong-willed targets, or manipulate environmental elements on a grand scale.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: The Force becomes an extension of your body. You move objects the size of vehicles, dominate minds that should be shielded, or reshape terrain. The line between Force user and natural disaster blurs.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: Reality bends to your will. You manipulate matter on a molecular level, overwhelm the mental defenses of trained Force-resisters, or create environmental effects that span city blocks.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: Transcendent Force projection. You move objects of impossible mass — starships, buildings, geological formations. You rewrite minds so completely that the target believes they have always thought this way. This is Starkiller pulling a Star Destroyer from orbit. This is the full, terrifying scope of the Force unleashed.

name: Charm
rule: The practice of winning favor, establishing trust, and using flattery to defuse a tense situation without resorting to violence.
guide: The Outer Rim is full of very angry, very paranoid people with very large guns. Charm is the incredibly valuable skill of making those people decide they'd rather buy you a drink than shoot you in the chest. It's not about being "nice"—it's about survival through aggressive politeness.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You make a decent impression. The target does not dislike you, and you have opened the door to further conversation. No favors yet, but no hostility either.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The target warms to you genuinely. You establish rapport, earn a measure of trust, and create a social opening that can be leveraged. They want to help you.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You win people over completely. Hostile targets become friendly. Suspicious targets become trusting. You create genuine emotional connections in minutes that normally take months.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your charisma is magnetic. People restructure their priorities to accommodate you. Strangers treat you like a lifelong friend. Doors open that have been closed for years.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You inspire devotion in people who have every reason to distrust you. Enemies lay down arms. Rivals become allies. Your words carry the weight of absolute sincerity even when they should not.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You reshape social reality. Communities rally behind you. Movements form around your words. Your charm transcends individual interaction and becomes a force that moves populations. This is the speech that stops a war.

name: Deception
rule: The art of lying, running long-cons, creating false identities, and bluffing your way past security checkpoints. Usually opposed by the enemy's Insight.
guide: Looking an Imperial Customs Officer dead in the eye and casually explaining that the three crates of illegal spice in your cargo hold are actually "rare medicinal herbs for your sick grandmother." It takes a sociopath or a professional. Usually both. If you're going to lie, make it big, and make it fast.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: The lie holds, but barely. The target accepts it for now, but a follow-up question or a moment of reflection might unravel it.
narrativeTiers.masterful: The lie is seamless. The target accepts it as truth without hesitation. Your cover story has no obvious gaps, your body language betrays nothing.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You construct elaborate false realities that survive scrutiny. False identities hold up under investigation. Planted evidence tells the story you want told. The truth becomes whatever you say it is.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your deceptions operate on a level that redefines the concept of truth for your targets. They do not merely believe your lie — they remember corroborating evidence that does not exist.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You deceive people who should be un-deceivable. Intelligence professionals, trained interrogators, and Force-sensitive truth-readers accept your fabrications as baseline reality.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You rewrite consensus reality. Organizations act on information you fabricated. Historical records reflect events you invented. The lie becomes the truth because no one alive remembers otherwise.

name: Insight
rule: The ability to read body language, detect falsehoods, determine a target's emotional state, or realize you are being walked into a trap.
guide: Everyone is lying to you. The bartender is lying about the watered-down drinks, the mechanic is lying about the hyperdrive motivator, and the client is definitely lying about the payout. Insight is how you catch the twitch in their eye before the knife goes in your back. Trust is for people with short lifespans.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You notice that something is off — a hesitation, a forced smile, a rehearsed answer. You know the target is not being fully honest, but you cannot pinpoint the specific deception.
narrativeTiers.masterful: You read the target clearly. You identify the lie, the motivation behind it, and the emotional state driving their behavior. Their poker face fails against your scrutiny.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You perceive the complete emotional and motivational landscape of your target. Hidden agendas surface. Buried fears become visible. You understand them better than they understand themselves.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: You read people the way others read open books. Micro-expressions, vocal patterns, postural shifts — you process them all simultaneously and construct a complete psychological profile in seconds.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You perceive intentions that the target has not yet consciously formed. You predict behavior before the target decides to act. Your insight borders on mind-reading.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You understand the complete truth of any person you observe. Every lie, every motive, every buried trauma and hidden strength — laid bare in a single moment of perfect clarity. No mask survives your gaze.

name: Persuasion
rule: The application of logical arguments, haggling over bounties, brokering truces, or convincing someone that your plan is in their best interest.
guide: The galaxy runs on credits and favors. Persuasion isn't about being a "nice guy"; it's about explaining exactly why it is in the other guy's best financial or physical interest to do exactly what you say. It's making them think your idea was actually their idea.

narrativeTiers.fleeting: You make a reasonable argument. The target acknowledges your point but is not yet moved to action. The seed is planted, but has not taken root.
narrativeTiers.masterful: Your argument is compelling. The target shifts their position, agrees to your terms, or concedes the point. You have changed their mind through logic and rhetoric.
narrativeTiers.legendary: You negotiate outcomes that should be impossible. Hostile parties agree to terms that favor you. Entrenched positions crumble under the weight of your reasoning.
narrativeTiers.unleashedI: Your arguments restructure how people think about the problem. They do not just agree — they cannot imagine having ever disagreed. Your logic rewires their framework.
narrativeTiers.unleashedII: You persuade people to act against their own established interests and feel good about it. Treaties that should take months are negotiated in hours. Feuds that span generations end with a conversation.
narrativeTiers.unleashedIII: You reshape the political and social landscape through pure rhetorical force. Governments change policy. Factions realign. Your words carry the weight of inevitability. This is the negotiation that redraws the map.

name: [Guarded X]
fiction: An established defensive stance or cover position — your body is correctly positioned to absorb incoming force.
rule: Physical protection from an established defensive stance or position. PC: Reduce incoming Melee Effect by X Tiers (minimum Tier 0). NPC: Reduce PC Melee Effect by X Tiers (minimum Tier 0). Does not protect against Ranged attacks. Each instance tracked with source, duration, and recovery.
guide: The best offense is a really, really impenetrable defense. [Guarded] doesn't mean you are invincible — it means that when someone throws their best shot at you, some of that effort just evaporates against your stance.

name: [Disoriented]
fiction: Physical coordination disrupted — a flash, concussion blast, inner ear damage, or sensory overload has compromised your body's ability to function precisely.
rule: Physical coordination compromised. PC: Step Down the Control Die on physical rolls (Physique, Reflex-based actions). NPC: Tier -1 (physical actions). Does not stack with itself. Superseded by [Blinded]. Combined with [Rattled] to form [Stunned].
pcEffect: Step down Control die on physical rolls
guide: Your hands aren't listening to your brain. A flash grenade, a concussion blast, a hard knock to the skull — something has scrambled the connection between intention and execution. Your aim is off, your footwork is wrong, and everything physical is a half-step behind where it should be.

name: [Rattled]
fiction: Mental focus broken — fear, confusion, demoralization, or psychological shock has disrupted your ability to think clearly and act decisively.
rule: Mental focus compromised. PC: Step Down the Control Die on mental and social rolls (Grit, Wits, Presence-based actions). NPC: Tier -1 (mental/social actions). Does not stack with itself. Combined with [Disoriented] to form [Stunned].
pcEffect: Step down Control die on mental/social rolls
guide: Your head isn't in the fight. Fear, confusion, a terrifying display of violence — something has cracked your composure. You can still swing a blade or pull a trigger, but trying to think, persuade, or keep your nerve? That's where the cracks show.

name: [Optimized]
fiction: Everything has clicked into place — position, angle, timing. A state of tactical or psychological advantage.
rule: Tactical advantage. PC: Step Up the Control Die (source specifies scope — a single Arena, specific action, or Universal). Does not stack with itself. NPC: Tier +1 (scoped). Each instance tracked with source, scope, duration, and recovery.
guide: Everything clicks. The angle is perfect, the target is wide open, and you can see exactly what needs to happen. Don't waste it.

name: [Weakened (Arena)]
fiction: Output compromised in a specific arena — injury, fatigue, a Force drain, or environmental pressure has reduced your effective power in one domain.
rule: Power output compromised. PC: Step Down the Power Die for the specified Arena. Multiple [Weakened] on the same Arena do not stack. NPC: Specified arena rating -1. Source specifies which arena is affected and recovery method.
guide: Something has taken the edge off your output in one area. A dislocated shoulder weakens Physique. A broken spirit weakens Grit. A scrambled neural link weakens Wits. The source determines what's weakened and what fixes it.

name: [Empowered (Arena)]
fiction: A stim, battle surge, Force technique, or perfect angle has amplified your offensive output beyond baseline.
rule: Power output boosted. PC: Step Up the Power Die for the specified Arena. Does not stack with itself on the same Arena. NPC: Specified arena rating +1. Source specifies scope and recovery.
guide: Something gave you an edge — a stim, a battle-surge, a Force trick, a perfect angle of attack. You hit harder. Make it count before it wears off.

name: [Shaken]
fiction: Your confidence is broken — demoralization, despair, or overwhelming dread has reduced the effectiveness of everything you do. The damage isn't physical, it's willpower.
rule: Outgoing effectiveness reduced. PC: Reduce outgoing Effect by 1 Tier on all actions. NPC: Reduce outgoing Effect by 1 Tier on all actions. Applies to everything while active — attacks, maneuvers, social actions. Does not stack with itself.
pcEffect: Reduce outgoing Effect by 1 tier all actions
npcEffect: Reduce outgoing Effect by 1 tier all actions
guide: You're not injured. You're not confused. You're just... less. Everything you do lands a little softer, a little weaker. Your will to push through has been cracked. Shake it off or accept diminished returns on everything.

name: [Exposed]
fiction: A tactical gap — your guard is down, your back is turned, your weapon is out of line. This is real geometric vulnerability, not a pain response.
rule: Defenses open against a specific threat type. Source specifies scope: Melee, Ranged, all attacks, or social/Force. PC: Incoming attacks of the specified type Step Up 1 Tier. NPC: PC actions of the specified type Step Up 1 Tier. Does not stack — multiple [Exposed] to the same type do not compound.
pcEffect: Incoming specified attacks step up 1 tier
npcEffect: PC specified attacks step up 1 tier
guide: Your guard is down. Your back is turned. There's a gap in your plating, your stance is wrong, or someone just knocked your weapon aside. Whatever the reason, the next person who hits you is going to hit you significantly harder.

name: [Pinned]
fiction: On the ground and physically held there — not just down, but locked down. Gravity, mass, and leverage are not negotiable.
rule: Combined condition: [Prone] + [Restrained] from a single source, with shared duration and recovery. The mechanical effects of both component conditions apply. Cannot move, [Exposed] to all Melee attacks. Clearing [Pinned] clears both components. Source determines duration and recovery.
guide: You're not just down — you're held down. Someone's knee is on your chest, binders are locked, or a Wookiee has you in a bear hug on the floor. Your only way out is to fight your way back up.

name: [Prone]
fiction: You are on the ground. Gravity put you there. Standing up takes effort.
rule: Knocked to the ground. PC: [Exposed] to all Melee attacks. Standing costs a Maneuver. NPC: [Exposed] to all Melee attacks. Standing costs an action. Applies identically to both.
guide: The deck is closer than you expected. You can't move freely, and anyone with a vibro-blade now has a significant geometric advantage over your skull. Get up. Now.

name: [Hazard X]
fiction: Something is actively damaging your body — fire, acid, electricity, poison. The physical damage is real and continues regardless of pain tolerance.
rule: Damage over time. PC and NPC: Deal X Vitality damage at the start of each turn. Source specifies the type (burn, acid, poison, etc.) and recovery method. Damage applies automatically.
guide: Something is actively killing you over time. Fire, acid, poison, or a leaking artery. It does not stop because you're busy. Address it or start counting down.

name: [Cover X]
fiction: A physical obstacle is between you and incoming fire. The geometry of the battlefield is working in your favor.
rule: Physical protection against Ranged attacks. When a PC has [Cover X], reduce the attacker's Power by X on ranged attacks against that PC. When an NPC has [Cover X], increase the NPC's Defense by X vs ranged attacks. Cover 1 = low wall or crate. Cover 2 = reinforced pillar or heavy debris. Cover 3 = armored barricade or vehicle hull. Cover 4 = fortified emplacement. Cover 5 = nearly total cover, blast door with firing slit. Does not protect against Melee attacks. Maintenance: Cover must be actively maintained each round by spending a Maneuver pip to hold your position. If you cannot or choose not to maintain, your Cover degrades to [Cover 1] at the start of your next turn — you are still behind the obstacle but no longer managing your angles.
guide: Blaster bolts travel in straight lines. Durasteel crates do not care about your feelings. Get behind something solid and let X do the work.

name: [Buffered X]
fiction: A temporary layer of protection — a stim-surge, a Force aura, jury-rigged armor — absorbing damage before it reaches your body.
rule: Temporary damage absorption. PC and NPC: Incoming damage depletes the Buffer first. When Buffer reaches 0 it is removed. Does not carry over between scenes unless source states otherwise.
guide: Think of it as a stim-pack that absorbs hits instead of healing them. It's not real health — it's borrowed time. Use it aggressively.

name: [Blinded]
fiction: Your visual input is gone — smoke, sand, a spiked visor, or physical damage to the eyes. This is an environmental or physical reality.
rule: Visual input compromised. PC: Cannot make targeted Ranged attacks. Step Down the Control Die on all physical actions. Supersedes [Disoriented]. NPC: Cannot make targeted Ranged attacks. Tier -1 (physical actions). Supersedes [Disoriented].
pcEffect: No targeted ranged. Step down Control die all physical
guide: Visor spiked, smoke in your eyes, sand in your face — doesn't matter how it happened. What matters is you can't aim at what you can't see.

name: [Shut Down]
fiction: Hardware failure — the droid's systems have been forced offline. This is a machine state, not a biological pain response.
rule: Droid characters only. Fully incapacitated — cannot take any actions. Source determines duration and recovery (typically a Tech check).
guide: The lights are off. The servos locked. Whatever was running in that tin skull just stopped. It's not dead — it's rebooting.

name: [Restrained]
fiction: Binders, a tractor beam, a Wookiee's grip — you are physically locked in place. The restraint exists in the physical world.
rule: Movement fully locked — still upright but going nowhere. PC: Cannot move. Escaping requires a successful opposed check or specific ability. NPC: Cannot move or reposition. Supersedes [Slowed].
guide: Something has physically stopped you from going anywhere. You're still on your feet, but you're not going anywhere without breaking free first.

name: [Suppressed]
fiction: You are engaged at range — an attacker has you in their sights. Moving from your current position means presenting yourself as a target.
rule: Engaged at range. PC and NPC: The target cannot leave their zone without presenting an Opening to the suppressor, who may Exploit it to attack. The suppressor must maintain line of sight. Source determines duration and recovery.
guide: Someone out there has you locked down with fire. You can still act — you just can't move without giving them a free shot. Stay put and fight back, or accept the risk and run.

name: [Bleeding]
fiction: Active blood loss — a wound that is open and draining. The body is losing resources it cannot afford to lose.
rule: Active blood loss. PC and NPC: Suffer 1 Vitality damage at the start of each turn. Source determines recovery method (typically Medicine check or bacta-patch).
guide: That wasn't a graze. Something opened up and the color red is now very much in play. You'll keep losing ground every single turn until someone patches you up.

name: [Stunned]
fiction: A concussive impact has disrupted both body and mind — nervous system scrambled, thoughts lagging. Everything is compromised.
rule: Combined condition: [Disoriented] + [Rattled] from a single source, with shared duration and recovery. The mechanical effects of both component conditions apply and stack naturally. Physical actions: -1 from [Disoriented]. Mental/social actions: -1 from [Disoriented] AND -1 from [Rattled] = -2 total. Clearing [Stunned] clears both components.
pcEffect: Step down Control die on ALL rolls (physical -1, mental/social -2)
guide: Your head is ringing. The lights are too bright. Everything is slightly behind where it should be. Body and mind both took the hit. You're still in the fight — you just need a moment your enemy is absolutely not going to give you.

name: [Surprised]
fiction: Caught completely off guard — the enemy struck before you knew they were there. Your coordination is shot and your defenses are wide open.
rule: Combined condition: [Disoriented] + [Exposed] applied automatically at the start of combat when caught unaware. Lasts until the end of your first turn. The mechanical effects of both component conditions apply. Cannot act during the surprise round. Clearing [Surprised] clears both components at end of first turn.
pcEffect: Step down Control die on physical rolls + incoming attacks step up 1 tier (until end of first turn)
npcEffect: Tier -1 (physical) + PC attacks step up 1 tier (until end of first turn)
guide: You didn't see it coming. The ambush, the betrayal, the thing in the shadows — it got the drop on you. Your body hasn't caught up to the danger yet. By the time your first turn ends, the adrenaline kicks in and the components clear. Until then, you're reacting late and exposed.

name: [Marked]
fiction: Someone has designated you as a specific target — a contract, a tactical designation, a Force-user's focus. No inherent pain or physical change.
rule: Personal combat only. No inherent mechanical effect. A tag that identifies a target for abilities, traits, or gambits that specifically interact with it. Source defines what [Marked] enables, its duration, and recovery. For starship-scale pursuit, see [Locked On].
guide: Someone has their eye on you. It doesn't slow you down, it doesn't hurt — yet. It just means someone out there has made you a specific project.

name: [Locked On]
fiction: An enemy pilot has won the positional contest and established pursuit lock. They are on your tail — they know your evasion patterns, their gunners have the angle, and your defensive options are compromised.
rule: Starship combat only. Established by winning a Dogfight and choosing the 'press' option. One-directional, single target — a ship can only have [Locked On] on one target at a time, but any number of ships can have [Locked On] on the same target. While [Locked On]: the locked-on ship's Evade reactions are [Weakened] against the locking ship; the locking ship's Gunners gain [Optimized] against the locked-on target. Cleared when the locked-on ship wins a Dogfight and chooses the disengage option, or when the locking Pilot takes an action that abandons the engagement (Evasive Maneuvers or Terrain Run outside a Dogfight). Both ships are engaged in the same zone while the Dogfight persists — neither can Fly or Attack Run until someone disengages.
pcEffect: Evade reactions [Weakened] against the locking ship
npcEffect: Locking ship's attacks gain [Optimized] against this target
guide: Someone is on your six. They won the turning fight, they matched your vector, and they know how you fly. Your evasive tricks are less effective against them specifically, and their gunners have the angle. The only way out is to win the Dogfight back and choose to break free — or make them regret following you into that asteroid field.

name: [Slowed]
fiction: Your movement is physically compromised — a damaged leg, difficult terrain, or mechanical failure.
rule: Movement impaired. PC: Cannot move more than 1 Zone per round. Moving while Engaged presents an Opening. NPC: Cannot move more than 1 Zone per round. Superseded by [Restrained]. Does not stack.
guide: Something is wrong with your legs, your drive system, or the ground itself. You can still move — just not fast enough to matter.

name: [Incapacitated]
fiction: You are completely out of the fight — unconscious, overwhelmed by pain, or shut down by a stun effect. The body has hit its limit. You are not dead, but you are not participating.
rule: Fully unable to act. PC and NPC: Cannot take any actions, maneuvers, or Exploits. Cannot move. Automatically fails all defense rolls. Source determines duration and recovery method (time, Medicine check, ally action, etc.). Does not stack — you are either incapacitated or you are not.
guide: The lights went out. Maybe it was a stun bolt, maybe it was a concussion wave, maybe your body just decided it had taken enough punishment and pulled the emergency shutdown on your brain. Whatever caused it, you are out of the fight until something or someone brings you back. Hope your friends are having a better day than you are.

name: [Stimmed X]
fiction: Your body has been chemically supported — bacta compounds, adrenaline analogs, coagulant agents. Each dose compounds on the last.
rule: Stacking condition. Each use of a Medpac or Stim Pack increases Stimmed rating by 1. Each point of Stimmed steps down the Medicine Power die by one size on future treatment rolls for this character (D12→D10→D8→D6→D4). At D4, further medical treatment has no meaningful effect — the body is chemically saturated. Clears fully during a Long Rest.
guide: Every stim compounds. The first medpac is easy. The second is tricky. By the fourth, even the best field medic is working with a body so full of competing chemicals that the dice barely move. Rest is the only real reset.

name: Natural Recovery
fiction: The body heals on its own. No medpac, no Medicine check, no Force trance — just time and rest. This is the baseline. Everything else builds on it.
rule: Short Rest (~1 hour, safe location): Recover 1 Trauma. No Vitality recovery. Long Rest (~8 hours, safe location): Recover 2 Trauma. Recover 1 Vitality. Treat Injury enhances these baselines — a successful Medicine roll during rest increases Trauma and Vitality recovery beyond what the body does alone. [Stimmed] clears fully on Long Rest only.
guide: Natural recovery is slow and incomplete by design. A crew that pushes through three firefights without resting will feel it — Vitality drops, Trauma accumulates, and the medpacs start running low. Rest is not downtime. Rest is a tactical decision.

name: Designing Vocations
fiction: A vocation is not a class. It is a lived identity — something a character has practiced, suffered through, and internalized until it changed how they move through the galaxy. Vocations do not teach characters new disciplines. They teach characters how to use the disciplines they already have in ways no one else would think of.
rule: A vocation is a 5-tier progression kit bound to one governing arena and one favored discipline. It grants the character a favored discipline (the discipline's die explodes on a max result — reroll and add — which is the key to reaching Unleashed tiers; additionally, the character may spend 1 Edge to reroll either their Control die or their Power die on any roll using a favored discipline). It provides a thematic identity and five abilities that escalate from foundational reframing to capstone mastery. Vocations are distinct from disciplines — disciplines are what you roll, vocations are how you think.
guide: This is the formal guide for creating and balancing vocations within the Kit System v2. Every design decision in a vocation should pass a single test: does this ability make the character feel like the vocation's identity, or does it just make them mechanically stronger? If the answer is the latter, redesign it.

structure.requirements: Every vocation must define: (1) an id following the pattern voc_[name], (2) a name (The [Noun]), (3) a governingArena (one of: physique, reflex, presence, wits, grit), (4) a favoredDiscipline (a single discipline that becomes favored for the character), (5) a description (what the vocation is, in plain language), (6) a fluff block (in-universe flavor — Star Wars characters who embody this archetype, the culture or tradition it comes from, the tone), (7) five abilities, one per tier.
structure.arenaAndDiscipline: The governing arena defines the vocation's mechanical axis — which attribute die it leans on. The favored discipline defines the vocation's signature skill. Together they answer: what does this character do, and what are they naturally good at? A vocation should never duplicate an existing vocation's arena + discipline pairing. Two vocations may share an arena (Gunslinger and Ghost both use Reflex) or share a discipline (theoretical), but never both. The combination is the vocation's mechanical fingerprint.
structure.naming: Vocations use 'The [Noun]' format. The name should evoke an identity, not a skill set. 'The Gunslinger' tells you who someone is. 'The Pistol Expert' tells you what they do. Prefer the former.

tierDesignPhilosophy.overview: The five tiers represent a progression from reframing to mastery. Each tier should feel like a meaningful evolution of the character's identity — not just a bigger number. A player reading Tier 1 through Tier 5 should understand who this person is becoming.

tier1.name: The Foundation — Identity Reframe
tier1.principle: Tier 1 establishes how this vocation sees the world differently. It does not add raw power — it changes the lens. The most common pattern is a discipline substitution: using one discipline in place of another for a specific context. This is not a free upgrade — it is a trade. The character gains access to a new approach but loses the original discipline's gambits in that context. The substituted discipline's gambits apply instead, creating a genuine choice in character investment.
patterns: Discipline Substitution — 'You may use [Discipline A] in place of [Discipline B] for [specific context].' The character reads situations, defends, or interacts through their vocation's lens rather than the default. Example: Noble uses Charm instead of Intimidate. Investigator uses Investigation instead of Insight.

Failure Mitigation — 'Even on a failed [action], you still gain [minor benefit].' The vocation is so ingrained that even a bad roll produces something. This should be a small narrative concession, never a mechanical save. Example: Investigator still learns one detail on a failed Assess.

Permission Removal — 'You no longer suffer [penalty] when [doing vocation-appropriate thing].' Removes a rule that punishes the vocation's core behavior. Example: Gunslinger does not present an Opening when firing while Engaged.

Passive Combat Shift — 'When [condition], gain [minor advantage] on your next [vocation action].' A reactive bonus that rewards the character for being in their element. Example: Shockboxer gains [Optimized] after taking Stun damage.
tier1.balance: Tier 1 should never grant [Optimized] or [Empowered] unconditionally. It should never grant a new action or maneuver. It should feel like a subtle but permanent shift in how the character operates. If Tier 1 is exciting on its own, it is probably too strong — it should be exciting because of what it enables at higher tiers.

tier2.name: The Technique — Signature Move or Tracking System
tier2.principle: Tier 2 gives the character their first active tool — something they can choose to do that no one else can. This is either a new maneuver (a rolled action with an Effect Track), a tracking mechanic (a passive system that rewards sustained engagement), or a passive that deepens the Tier 1 reframe with meaningful new capability.
patterns: Signature Maneuver — A rolled maneuver using the vocation's favored discipline and governing arena. Should have a clear Effect Track (Fleeting/Masterful/Legendary) with escalating outcomes. The maneuver should be useful but not dominant — it is a tool, not a win button. Example: Noble's Read the Room (Charm Assess with social bonuses).

Tracking Mechanic — A passive counter or state machine that rewards the character for doing what the vocation does. Must have a clear trigger, a visible accumulation, and a payoff threshold. The tracker should reset at the end of the character's turn or scene to prevent runaway stacking. Example: Shockboxer's Combo Counter (track consecutive Brawl hits, unlock conditions at thresholds).

Deepening Passive — A passive that adds a new dimension to the Tier 1 ability without replacing it. Should feel like the character has practiced the foundation long enough to discover something new within it. Example: Investigator's Connect the Dots (spend Assess questions to propose theories instead of asking the GM).

Gambit — A vocation-specific gambit attached to an existing action type. Follows the standard gambit rules: costs -1 Tier, declared before rolling. Example: Ghost's Blind Spot (Dodge gambit, spend 1 tier for [Optimized] on next attack).
tier2.balance: Tier 2 is the vocation's workhorse. It should be something the player reaches for most sessions. If it is too situational, the vocation feels empty at mid-levels. If it is too powerful, it overshadows Tier 3. The test: would a player with only Tiers 1 and 2 feel like the vocation is already delivering on its promise? The answer should be yes — just not completely.

tier3.name: The Commitment — Defining Capability
tier3.principle: Tier 3 is the vocation's centerpiece. This is the ability that defines the vocation in play — when someone describes what their character does, this is the ability they are thinking of. It should be a significant new capability that clearly separates this character from anyone who does not have this vocation.
patterns: Signature Maneuver (if not at T2) — If Tier 2 was a passive or gambit, Tier 3 is where the vocation's signature rolled ability lives. This maneuver should have a full Effect Track and a meaningful risk on failure. Example: Ghost's Slip (Stealth maneuver for concealment), Noble's Compel (Charm vs Resolve social pressure), Investigator's Keen Insight (Investigation reconstruction).

Gambit — A vocation gambit that modifies a core action type (Attack, Defense, or existing maneuver). Should be more impactful than a Tier 2 gambit — broader scope, stronger effect, or enabling a new tactical pattern. Example: Shockboxer's Body Work (Brawl attack gambit, hit counts as 2 for Combo Counter).

Major Passive with Attached Gambit — A passive system that introduces new mechanical infrastructure (like a persistent object, a summonable entity, or a new resource) and attaches a gambit to an existing action. Example: Ichor Witch's Ichor Infusion (two talismans + Blight gambit on infused weapons).

Narrative Maneuver — A rolled maneuver that produces narrative information rather than mechanical effects. Still uses the vocation's discipline and arena, still has an Effect Track, but the tiers describe what the character learns or reconstructs rather than what conditions are applied. The GM must confirm preconditions before the roll occurs. Example: Investigator's Keen Insight is a rolled Investigation (Wits) maneuver, but the GM confirms the target is narratively significant before the dice hit the table.
tier3.balance: Tier 3 is where balance matters most. This ability will see the most use and generate the most table-time. Apply these checks: (1) Does the ability have a real failure state or cost? If success is guaranteed, it is too strong. (2) Does the ability step on an existing discipline's territory? If a discipline gambit already does something similar, the vocation ability must do it differently or in a different context. (3) Can the ability be spammed? If it can fire every turn with no diminishing returns, add a limiter — per-scene, per-target immunity on failure, action cost, or Edge cost.

tier4.name: The Upgrade — Power Spike or New Mode
tier4.principle: Tier 4 takes something the vocation already does and makes it significantly better, or introduces a new mode of engagement that builds on the existing kit. This is not a new identity — it is the old identity at a higher gear.
patterns: Direct Upgrade — 'Upgrades [Tier 2 or 3 ability].' Adds a new trigger, extends duration, adds a secondary effect, or removes a limitation. The upgrade should reference the earlier ability by name and specify exactly what changes. Example: Investigator's Follow the Thread upgrades Keen Insight (Masterful 8+ grants a free Assess at +1 Tier).

Defensive Gambit — A gambit attached to a defense action (Dodge, Endure, Resist) that creates a counter-attack opportunity or repositioning advantage. Example: Shockboxer's Roll With It (Brawl defense gambit, attacker presents an Opening).

Persistent Resource — A new persistent entity or resource that the character manages across scenes. Must have clear creation rules, limits (usually 1-2 active at a time), and interaction rules with the rest of the kit. Example: Ichor Witch's Ichor Talisman is established at T2 but the second talisman slot opens at T3, showing how persistence can escalate across tiers.

Exploit Modifier — A new way to spend the Exploit pip that replaces or enhances the default reaction. Exploit abilities at Tier 4 reward the character for creating or receiving Openings through earlier-tier abilities. The trigger should be specific (a particular defense type, a particular condition on the target). Example: Ghost's Vanishing Act (on Dodge with Control 8+, spend Exploit pip to gain concealment).

Passive Power — A passive that is simply strong. At this tier, a passive can grant [Optimized] conditionally, auto-include allies, or provide narrative permissions that bypass normal rules. The condition must be specific enough that it is not always active. Example: Noble's Entourage (declare an NPC ally in social scenes, [Optimized] when they visibly support you).
tier4.balance: Tier 4 is a reward for investment. It should feel powerful — but the power should be channeled through the vocation's identity, not bolted on. A Tier 4 ability that would be equally useful on any vocation is poorly designed. The test: if you moved this ability to a different vocation, would it still make sense? If yes, it is too generic.

tier5.name: The Capstone — Mastery
tier5.principle: Tier 5 is the statement. This is the ability that says: this character has mastered this vocation. It should be the most dramatic, the most impactful, and the most memorable ability in the kit. It should also be the most constrained — capstones that fire every round are not capstones, they are passives wearing a cape.
patterns: Upgrade with Extended Reach — Upgrades a Tier 3 maneuver to affect groups, extend duration, or chain into additional actions. The upgrade should feel like the vocation's philosophy applied at scale. Example: Noble's Voice of Authority (Compel now targets groups, Masterful 8+ extends influence through the Act).

Cascade Effect — When the vocation's signature action succeeds, it triggers benefits for the entire crew. The character's mastery lifts everyone around them. Example: Gunslinger's Two for Flinching (Exploiting an Opening with a Pistol creates a new Opening for an ally). Investigator's The Confrontation (spend 1 Edge for crew-wide [Optimized] + [Empowered] on Join Battle).

Scene-Ending Declaration — A once-per-scene or once-per-act ability that resolves a situation outright if specific conditions are met. The conditions should be difficult to engineer, requiring setup from earlier tiers. Example: Shockboxer's The Knockout (Combo Counter 3+ and Masterful Brawl attack instantly incapacitates). Ghost's Exit Strategy (all allies concealed + none restrained = the crew escapes).

Apex Action — A dramatic new action that represents the pinnacle of the vocation's capability. Should have high risk, high reward, and significant cost (Edge, Hope/Toll flip, or leaving the character vulnerable). Example: Ichor Witch's True Possession (full spirit projection into a target, body goes Helpless, costs 1 Edge per round to sustain).
tier5.balance: Capstones should pass the 'story test' — when this fires at the table, does it create a moment people will remember? If the answer is 'it is mechanically efficient but not exciting,' redesign it. Capstones come in two valid forms: constrained (once per scene, once per act, or requiring a board state the character must engineer) and cascading (always-on but dependent on the character first succeeding at what the vocation already does). Gunslinger's Two for Flinching is cascading — it fires every time the character Exploits an Opening with a Pistol and deals damage, but the Opening must be earned and the damage must land. The power is real but conditional on execution. A capstone that is both always-on AND unconditional is not a capstone — it is a passive wearing a cape.

abilityTypes.overview: Vocations may use the following ability types. Each type has specific rules for how it integrates with the action economy and the existing mechanical systems. Choosing the right type for each tier is as important as designing the effect itself.
abilityTypes.passive: Always active. No action cost, no declaration. The ability simply changes how the character interacts with the system. Passives should not grant unconditional dice bonuses — they should change what is possible, not inflate what already works. Good passives reframe, substitute, track, or permit. Bad passives just add numbers.
abilityTypes.maneuver: A rolled action with a full Effect Track (Fleeting/Masterful/Legendary). Uses the vocation's favored discipline and governing arena. May have a defense target (vs Resolve, vs Evasion, etc.) or may be uncontested (Assess-style). Must define a risk on failure — what happens when the character overreaches. Maneuvers consume the character's Maneuver pip for the round. A vocation should have at most two maneuvers across its five tiers — more than that floods the action economy.
abilityTypes.action: A rolled action that consumes the character's Action pip for the round. More expensive than a maneuver in action economy terms.
